author: matkt0
content:
not an issue in the spec more missing tests . This one are benchmark tests I was hoping to have the same kind of tests in https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/releases/tag/tests-bal%40v7.0.0

I don't know if it is easy or not to add them but it will be better to have that in order to avoid regression in the code . from what I saw we don't have any tests like that on this part

author: nero_eth
content: What would that test be testing? That a client is able to pass the test fast enough, such that we can be assured it implements the parallelization correctly, and the test would fail if the execution speed is too slow?

author: matkt0
content: The test will verify that I obtain the correct state root if I execute two transactions with parallelization where an account or a slot is shared between two transactions. for example, tx 1 writes to slot A and tx 2 reads from slot A . If we don't have this, it means we are not really testing the parallelization logic with these tests, specifically the fact of applying the state changes (BALs) from previous transactions before running a transaction in parallel.

author: matkt0
content: In any case, I haven't seen a test doing this on my side. We can execute transactions correctly without parallelization and have a bug in the code that applies the BAL for transactions in parallel. Not a performance test, just a test to verify this part of the code.

author: nero_eth
content: yeah this feels like we could just copy one of the BAL-benchmarking tests over to the BAL tests. cc <@762919002196410400> <@796570016966770688> <@508125616940515329>

author: m0_ox
content: will add a few of these variations to the consensus tests, thanks <@680337202521440470> 👍🏽

author: m0_ox
content:
Put up some scenarios in a PR here: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2851. Important to include different senders, different scenarios of dependency, etc... so we know clients can't just use `nonce` checks if same sender to parallelize, etc. I think a lot of cases may be covered from previous fork tests when filled with Amsterdam but wanted to make sure we have some multi-tx tests that cover one tx `CREATE` and another sender access, one tx delegates and others trigger code in succession, multiple balance change pass-throughs for exact gas while the last tx uses that gas w/ a off-by-one OOG case as a sanity check.

Lmk in the PR if you think of some other cases that would be good to add.
